meta.question: Is there any cyclic behaviour in this model? (Model ID: 0b21c69687b64d8886fb7cc5dc56ff6c)
meta.finalAnswer: No—using the model’s sequence-flow graph in choreography “0b21c69687b64d8886fb7cc5dc56ff6c”, I could not find any node that can reach itself via one or more outgoing bbo:SequenceFlow edges (i.e., no detectable directed cycles).

request.query:
prefix bbo: <http://www.onto-bpo.eu/ontologies/bbo#>
prefix bboExt: <http://www.onto-bpo.eu/ontologies/bboExtension#>
prefix rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
prefix tai: <https://www.teamingai-project.eu/>
prefix xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT
  (COUNT(DISTINCT ?node) AS ?nodesInChoreo)
  (COUNT(DISTINCT ?cycleFrom) AS ?cycleEdges)
  ?choreo
WHERE {
  ?model a bboExt:Model ;
         tai:normalizedId "0b21c69687b64d8886fb7cc5dc56ff6c" .
  ?model tai:belongsTo ?org .
  ?defs tai:belongsTo ?model .
  ?choreo a bboExt:Choreography ;
          tai:belongsTo ?defs .

  # Detect any directed cycle in the sequence-flow graph of this choreography.
  # We look for a path from a node back to itself via one or more outgoing sequence-flow edges.
  ?start bbo:name ?startName .
  FILTER(?startName != "")

  ?start ((^bbo:has_sourceRef)/bbo:has_targetRef)+ ?start .
  BIND(?start AS ?cycleFrom)
  BIND(?choreo AS ?choreo)

  # Count distinct nodes involved (at least start nodes that have a cycle back to themselves)
  BIND(?start AS ?node)
}
GROUP BY ?choreo
LIMIT 25

request.message: Parse error: BIND: Variable used when already in-scope: ?choreo in BIND(?choreo AS ?choreo)
request.reason: The query failed due to an RDF/SPARQL parse error caused by a redundant BIND where the target variable already exists. I will simplify the cycle-detection query by removing that BIND and ensuring grouping variables are used consistently.

request.query:
prefix bbo: <http://www.onto-bpo.eu/ontologies/bbo#>
prefix bboExt: <http://www.onto-bpo.eu/ontologies/bboExtension#>
prefix rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
prefix tai: <https://www.teamingai-project.eu/>
prefix xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT
  (COUNT(DISTINCT ?node) AS ?nodesInChoreo)
  (COUNT(DISTINCT ?start) AS ?cyclicStartNodes)
  (COUNT(DISTINCT ?edge) AS ?sequenceFlowEdges)
  ?choreo
WHERE {
  ?model a bboExt:Model ;
         tai:normalizedId "0b21c69687b64d8886fb7cc5dc56ff6c" .
  ?model tai:belongsTo ?org .
  ?defs tai:belongsTo ?model .
  ?choreo a bboExt:Choreography ;
          tai:belongsTo ?defs .

  # Nodes that can reach themselves via at least one outgoing sequence-flow edge.
  ?start bbo:name ?startName .
  FILTER(BOUND(?startName) && STRLEN(STR(?startName)) > 0)

  ?start ((^bbo:has_sourceRef)/bbo:has_targetRef)+ ?start .

  BIND(?start AS ?node)
  OPTIONAL {
    ?edge a bbo:SequenceFlow ;
          bbo:has_sourceRef ?node .
  }
}
GROUP BY ?choreo
LIMIT 25

request.message: No cyclic start nodes were found under the assumption that cycles must begin at nodes with non-empty bbo:name and that a path closure via outgoing sequence-flows returns to the same node.
request.reason: This could be a false negative due to nodes lacking bbo:name or due to cycle structure involving unnamed nodes. I will retry with a more general definition: detect any node in the choreography that can reach itself, without requiring a name, and I will count all nodes involved rather than only named starts.

request.query:
prefix bbo: <http://www.onto-bpo.eu/ontologies/bbo#>
prefix bboExt: <http://www.onto-bpo.eu/ontologies/bboExtension#>
prefix rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
prefix tai: <https://www.teamingai-project.eu/>
prefix xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT
  (COUNT(DISTINCT ?node) AS ?cyclicNodes)
  (COUNT(DISTINCT ?start) AS ?startNodes)
  (COUNT(DISTINCT ?choreo) AS ?choreoCount)
  ?choreo
WHERE {
  ?model a bboExt:Model ;
         tai:normalizedId "0b21c69687b64d8886fb7cc5dc56ff6c" .
  ?model tai:belongsTo ?org .
  ?defs tai:belongsTo ?model .
  ?choreo a bboExt:Choreography ;
          tai:belongsTo ?defs .

  # Any node that can reach itself via one or more outgoing sequence-flow edges
  ?start ((^bbo:has_sourceRef)/bbo:has_targetRef)+ ?start .

  # Constrain the nodes to be in this choreography container
  ?start tai:belongsTo ?choreo .

  BIND(?start AS ?node)
}
GROUP BY ?choreo
LIMIT 25

request.message: The generalized reachability query (self-reach via outgoing sequence flows) still found no cycles for the choreography(s) in the given model ID.
request.reason: All tested cycle-detection variants returned empty results, so within this dataset/representation there is no evidence of cyclic behaviour in the model’s sequence-flow graph. I will report that no cyclic self-reach was found for the given model ID.

request.answer: No—using the model’s sequence-flow graph in choreography “0b21c69687b64d8886fb7cc5dc56ff6c”, I could not find any node that can reach itself via one or more outgoing bbo:SequenceFlow edges (i.e., no detectable directed cycles).

events.finalAnswer: No—using the model’s sequence-flow graph in choreography “0b21c69687b64d8886fb7cc5dc56ff6c”, I could not find any node that can reach itself via one or more outgoing bbo:SequenceFlow edges (i.e., no detectable directed cycles).
